name: TAO 4h Compression Fakeout Re-Expansion v1
edge_mechanism: compression resolves one way, that move fails, and the opposite direction is entered once directional control reasserts with a simple confirmation sequence
thesis: Cycle 86 includes compression fakeout into re-expansion with directional confirmation. TAO 4h is the only lane in the basket where a second-leg expansion after a fake move has real structural room to run. The lesson from the recent sparse squeeze families is to keep confirmation simple and only test fakeout-to-go where trend contamination can actually carry the second move.
invalidation_condition: If TAO 4h fakeout-to-go sequences still produce 0-1 trades or the simplified confirmation enters too late to monetize, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Volatility compresses, one side breaks and fails, the opposite side takes control, and expansion follows.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Compression often traps premature breakout participation. When the first move fails in a trend-capable lane, the second move can travel farther because trapped inventory helps fuel it.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because it is the only lane in the basket where fakeout-to-go continuation has enough trend contamination to plausibly pay.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The fakeout may not be distinct enough, causing either late entry or undertrading.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If it trades but runners truncate, extend the trail before widening the compression detector.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Lower count than the reclaim ideas, but stronger asymmetry when the second-leg expansion is real.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h clears moderate density and the simplified fakeout confirmation produces materially more participation than recent dead persistence families.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or fakeout-confirmation entries that reverse immediately on most occurrences.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable if confirmation stays simple and lane fit stays trend-capable

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit trend-capable lane for compression fakeout into re-expansion.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h shows enough fakeout-to-go sequences with positive first-target expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h still returns near-zero trades or immediate reversals after confirmation.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with a minority of stronger expansion legs carrying the expectancy.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same fakeout-to-go grammar survives on a weaker but still transition-capable cross-lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: AXS 4h increases count while preserving fakeout-to-go structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: AXS 4h becomes noisy and loses the mechanism.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Higher count, smaller second-leg expansions than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Useful control to see whether the concept collapses when trend contamination disappears.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h still shows some orderly fakeout-to-go patterns.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the concept belongs on trend-capable lanes only.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Sparse and smaller expansions than TAO.

long: Time-based de-risk: cut 25% after 6 bars if no 0.95 ATR expansion
short: Time-based de-risk: cut 25% after 6 bars if no 0.95 ATR expansion
